cards.text: Malaysia narrowed its deficit to 4.1% in 2024 — but 2025 is forecast to slip past the 3.8% target as revenue softens.
cards.sub: The IMF and analyst houses note rising risks from weaker tax collection, lower oil prices, and SST expansion delays.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Every 1 percentage point of deficit adds tens of billions to annual borrowing.
cards.s: Long-term sustainability requires revenue mobilisation (broader SST, CGT) or sustained spending discipline. Both have political costs.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Credit rating outlooks are sensitive to consistent target overshoots — even a one-notch downgrade raises borrowing costs across the sovereign curve.
cards.s: Higher sovereign risk premium cascades through corporate bond markets and ultimately to mortgage rates and household credit costs.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: The Medium-Term Fiscal Framework sets targets but has no statutory enforcement mechanism for missing them.
cards.s: Parliament debates budget allocations but does not hold a structured post-budget accountability hearing on deficit outcomes.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: The story is not 'target missed' — it is whether Malaysia can establish a credible glide path before rating agencies decide consolidation is no longer credible.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Like a dieter who keeps just over the goal weight every quarter — the trend is right, the credibility eroding.
cards.sub: Persistent small overshoots accumulate into a credibility gap that markets eventually price in.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: A statutory Fiscal Responsibility framework with binding ceilings and corrective triggers — beyond the current MTFF — is the structural fix.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: Malaysia's 2024 Deficit Came In at 4.1% — 2025 Forecast Risks Slipping Past the 3.8% Target
